task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam main application window is visible on the screen.
A settings, configuration, or preferences dialog box for bcWebCam is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Locate and enable the auto-start scanning setting
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox or toggle option related to 'auto-start scanning', 'start automatically', or similar wording is checked or enabled within the settings dialog.

task_completion.description: Apply or save the settings changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is seen clicking an 'OK', 'Save', or 'Apply' button in the settings dialog, or the dialog is successfully closed after the setting was changed.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the main window to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or an image editing application (like Paint or Snipping Tool) is visible, saving an image file exactly named 'auto_start_configured.png' into the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam GUI to change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through the bcWebCam graphical interface rather than using command-line tools or scripts to modify application state.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the GUI by editing configuration files directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like Notepad) or terminal windows open showing direct edits to bcWebCam's underlying .ini, .xml, or registry configuration files.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely captured and saved a screenshot
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a screen capture tool or built-in OS screenshot functionality and saved it properly, rather than simply creating an empty or text file named 'auto_start_configured.png' using terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the screenshot is exactly C:\Users\Docker\Documents\auto_start_configured.png.
